Data Representation.title: Data Representation
Data Representation.content: Computers store text, images, sound, and numbers as binary patterns. Data representation covers ASCII, Unicode, binary arithmetic, and the different ways to encode information.

Decimal number system.title: Decimal Number System
Decimal number system.content: The decimal number system uses ten digits (0-9). It is the standard system for denoting integer and non-integer numbers. Converting between decimal and binary is a common skill in computer science.

Binary number system.title: Binary Number System
Binary number system.content: The binary number system uses two digits (0 and 1). It is the base-2 system used internally by digital computers. Practice converting decimal numbers to binary and vice versa.

Pseudocode.title: Pseudocode
Pseudocode.content: Pseudocode is a plain-language description of the steps in an algorithm. It uses structured statements that resemble programming languages but are easier to read and write for planning solutions.

Flowchart symbols.title: Updating
Flowchart symbols.content: Flowcharts use shapes such as rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and ovals for start/end. Learning these symbols helps you translate pseudocode into visual diagrams.
